sessions.lever: triage only — identified the branch-generation + $3/$5 swap coupling; no edit attempted (complex, 27-commit history)

Variant sweep: 41 forms tested. Best matched-count (bi=107) reached floor 4 via `kind_full=lhu; kind = kind_full & 0xFFFFU` split + if/else `t = (s32)*((u16*)+0x270) << 16` (else lui-folded). Best overall floor 2 (bi=108) reached via the kind-split + dual-typed read at +0x270 (`s32 probe=s16-read; s32 v=u16-read; if(probe>=4) v=3; t=v<<16`) yielding target's exact `lhu $a1; andi $v1, $a1, 0xFFFF; ...; addiu $v1,3; sll` shape — but with 1 extra `andi $v1,$v0,0xFFFF` insn at the ternary (because GCC CSE'd the u16 read into the s16-load value and materialized u16 via andi rather than emitting a separate lhu).

re-verified HEAD floor=11 (bi=107==target). Re-examined target asm at .L8001FA60: unambiguously emits `lh $v0, 0x270($a0); lhu $v1, 0x270($a0)` BOTH UNCONDITIONAL — i.e. the original C source MUST have had a dual-typed read at +0x270 (no other GCC 2.7.2 construct produces both lh and lhu of the same address). The previous session's reviewer-FAIL of the dual-typed-read variant is therefore in tension with what the target source provably did. Re-confirming needs-decision; no new lever attempted (would duplicate the 41-variant sweep).

reviewer.evidence: Cheat-reviewer FAILED v36 form (the floor-2 candidate). Deciding construct: unconditional dual-typed read of same address at +0x270 (`s32 probe = *((s16*)(arg0+0x270)); s32 v = *((u16*)(arg0+0x270));`) — fails Tests 1 (no semantic purpose; one variable would suffice) and 2 (human-programmer). Not covered by SOTN-accepted list nor split-init-accumulation sanctioning. Saved to `rejected/dual-type-probe-load.c`. Caveat: HEAD's pure-C body ALSO contains a dual-typed read at +0x270 (s16 probe for guard + u16 read INSIDE the ternary for the value when guard holds); the reviewer flagged the unconditional split-into-separate-variables form here but did not opine on HEAD's guarded form. The `kind_full & 0xFFFFU` split (used to provoke target's `lhu;andi` kind-load shape) was noted as codegen-steering but secondary; reviewer treated it as dual-use with split-init-accumulation.

remaining_gap.summary: 13 regfix rules: $3<->$5 reg-pair swap (4 places) + insert `andi $3,$5,0xFFFF` (kind narrow) + 7-rule subst/delete/insert_label cluster rewriting the saturating-ternary branch generation. The kind narrow (insert `andi`) AND the ternary's `addiu+sll` pattern CAN both be provoked from pure C via `kind = kind_full & 0xFFFFU` + dual-typed read at +0x270 — but the cheat-reviewer FAILS the dual-typed read. Without dual-typed read, GCC reuses the s16 load and emits `move $v1,$v0` (not the target's `lhu $v1`), keeping the floor at >=4.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: All 13. HEAD matches only with them. Best pure-C floor 4 (matched count) or 2 (extra-insn); neither close enough for byte-match.

next_hypotheses: USER DECISION REQUIRED: Is the dual-typed read at same address (s16+u16 at +0x270) — already in HEAD's pure-C body as a guarded form — a sanctioned technique for this function? If yes, ship v36 form (rejected/dual-type-probe-load.c) as candidate.c with rule retirement. If no, this function is structurally not closable in pure-C and warrants parking or canonical-asm authorization.

Alternative single-read variant (`s32 v = *((s16*)(+0x270)); if (v >= 4) v = 3; t = v<<16;` — no u16 read) was tried (v41) and scored 8 vs head 11; the simplification removes too many insns (bi=104) and diverges from target. Target genuinely uses both lh and lhu separately.
Volatile-qualified u16 read to defeat CSE was tried (v38) and scored 5 (worse than v30's 4); volatile changes load semantics and adds work elsewhere.

Two-pointer alias (`const s16 *p_s; const u16 *p_u; *p_s; *p_u`) was tried (v37); same as v36 — GCC's old-school CSE still merges. No alias-breaking technique in pure C reaches target without codegen-steering.
27-commit history; was 'retired' 2026-05-14 leaving these rules (a stabilizer, not COMPLETED-C). This session adds adversarial-review data: the dual-typed read appears load-bearing for matching.

rejected_forms.why: Unconditional dual-typed read at same address — codegen-steering with no semantic purpose. Cheat-reviewer default-FAIL.
